variations: 進階版：如果行李遺失，賠償金可以拿來辦健身房年費。
後續：機場海關問我那一圈要申報什麼，我說「免稅的肉」。

en.content:
I figured out a fast way to lose weight: check my fat as checked luggage.
Next time I fly, when the counter says I'm five kilos over, I'll peel the ring off my waist, slap a fragile sticker on it, and send it to the cargo hold.

I'll pick it up on the return flight. This way I don't even need travel insurance.
en.editorNote: Drop into the family chat the moment you book flights, paired with a photo of a lunch you definitely shouldn't have ordered
variations: Upgrade: if the luggage gets lost, the compensation pays for a yearly gym membership.
Follow-up: customs asked what I was declaring. I said 'duty-free meat'.

items.sourceNote: 卡路里追蹤 app 自欺欺人主題重寫，加入台灣便當與手搖飲情境

zh-tw.content:
我下載了卡路里計算 app，今天認真記錄：
早餐 300 大卡、午餐 500 大卡、晚餐 400 大卡。

附註欄寫著：手搖飲、雞排、宵夜、室友烤的餅乾、路過聞到的滷味，這些 app 沒問就不算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給跟你一起辦健身房又一起放棄的戰友——他會說這就是科學減肥
variations: 進階版：app 通知我今天熱量沒超標，我感動到去買了第二杯珍奶慶祝。
後續：客服回信說 app 沒辦法偵測「視覺攝取」，所以看別人吃不算。

en.content:
I downloaded a calorie tracking app and logged everything honestly today:
Breakfast 300, lunch 500, dinner 400.

In the notes I wrote: bubble tea, fried chicken cutlet, late-night snack, the cookies my roommate baked, the braised stuff I smelled walking past — if the app doesn't ask, it doesn't count.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who signed up for the gym with you and quit with you — they'll call this scientific dieting
variations: Upgrade: the app told me I stayed under my calorie limit today, so I bought a second bubble tea to celebrate.
Follow-up: support emailed back saying the app can't detect 'visual intake', so watching other people eat doesn't count.

items.sourceNote: 「Probably nothing arouses more false hopes than the first four hours of a diet」格式重寫，台灣口語延伸

zh-tw.content:
這個世界上最讓人燃起希望的時刻，是減肥的前四個小時。
那四個小時，你相信自己會在夏天前瘦十公斤、會買到 S 號的牛仔褲、會被前任後悔。

第五個小時開始，你只相信便利商店的鹹酥雞便當。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在早上立完減肥誓言、傍晚打開外送 app 的時候傳給自己
variations: 進階版：第六個小時，你已經在規劃明天要從哪一餐重新開始。
後續：減肥日記第一頁寫滿目標，第二頁開始就只寫菜單。

en.content:
The moment that fills people with the most hope in this world is the first four hours of a diet.
In those four hours, you believe you'll drop ten kilos before summer, fit into a size small, and make your ex regret everything.

Starting in hour five, the only thing you believe in is the fried-chicken bento at the convenience store.
en.editorNote: Send to yourself in the morning after the vow, and again at sunset when the delivery app opens
variations: Upgrade: by hour six, you're already planning which meal tomorrow you'll restart from.
Follow-up: page one of the diet journal is full of goals. From page two it's just menus.

items.sourceNote: 「seafood diet — I see food and I eat it」經典格式，改為台灣手搖飲與夜市情境

zh-tw.content:
我在執行一種叫「目視飲食法」的計畫。
看到食物，我就吃。沒看到，我就找。找不到，我就外送。

營養師說這沒什麼效果，但我覺得它的執行力是我這輩子最高的一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在打開外送 app 的時候傳到減肥群組，配一張你剛剛點完的明細截圖
variations: 進階版：我已經把這套方法寫成電子書，書名叫《看見就贏一半》。
後續：朋友問我能不能教他，我說可以，學費請用炸雞支付。

en.content:
I'm on something called the visual diet.
If I see food, I eat it. If I don't see it, I look for it. If I can't find it, I order delivery.

The nutritionist says it's not effective, but it's the most committed I've ever been to any plan in my life.
en.editorNote: Send to the diet group chat the moment you open the delivery app, paired with a screenshot of your order
variations: Upgrade: I've turned the method into an e-book called 'Seeing Is Half The Battle'.
Follow-up: a friend asked if I'd teach him. I said sure, tuition is payable in fried chicken.

items.sourceNote: 「lost the same ten pounds many times, cellulite has déjà vu」概念重寫，加入台灣體脂機情境

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子減下來的體重加起來大概有 80 公斤，
但目前還是同一個人。

體脂機看到我都會嘆氣，因為它覺得自己被當成電動玩具，每次都是同一關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在量完體重很沮喪的早上傳給也在減肥的閨蜜，附上一張早餐的拿鐵照
variations: 進階版：我家那台體脂機已經學會自動省略「身體年齡」那一行，怕我看了想退貨。
後續：健身房教練建議我換一台，我說問題不在機器，是我的迴圈程式跑不出去。

en.content:
Across my lifetime I've lost about eighty kilos in total,
but I'm still the same person.

My body composition scale sighs every time it sees me. It feels like a video game cartridge stuck on the same level.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's also dieting on a sad weigh-in morning, ideally with a latte photo attached
variations: Upgrade: my scale at home now auto-hides the 'body age' line because it's worried I'll return it.
Follow-up: my trainer suggested a new model. I told him the bug isn't in the hardware, it's in my loop.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm into fitness, fittin' this pizza in my mouth」雙關概念重寫，改為台灣鹹酥雞情境

zh-tw.content:
別人問我有沒有在運動，我說我很注重「肌動」。
他們以為我在練重訓，其實我說的是「雞動」——
看到鹹酥雞就會自動心跳加速、雙手伸出、口水分泌的那種運動。

燃脂效果普通，但這套動作我已經練到完全反射。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下班經過夜市的時候傳給戰友，配一句「你要加九層塔嗎」
variations: 進階版：我這套訓練不需要器材，鹹酥雞攤就是我的健身房。
後續：教練問我深蹲幾下，我說看老闆炸幾分鐘。

en.content:
When people ask if I work out, I say I'm really into fryness training.
They think I mean fitness — actually I mean the fried-chicken reflex.
The one where my heart rate spikes, my hands extend, and my salivary glands kick in the second I smell the stall.

Fat-burn results are mediocre, but I have the movement down to pure muscle memory.
en.editorNote: Send to your gym buddy on the walk home past the night market, with a 'extra basil?' tagged on
variations: Upgrade: my training needs zero equipment. The fried-chicken stall is my gym.
Follow-up: my coach asked how many squats I did. I said it depends on how long the oil's been heating.

items.sourceNote: 「gym membership card thinks we're making progress」概念重寫，加入台灣健身房月費情境

zh-tw.content:
我辦了健身房年費，月費自動扣款已經第八個月。
會員卡還在皮夾裡，狀態維持「全新」。

健身房櫃台小姐看到我來都很驚訝，問我「會員是不是要退費」，我說不用，我是來感受月費花掉的那一刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在月初被扣款通知吵醒的時候傳到家族群組，配一張你的健身房 APP 截圖
variations: 進階版：我覺得這筆錢不是運動費，是「我有在努力」的心理治療費。
後續：年底續約的時候，櫃台問我要不要改方案，我說維持原樣，這樣比較像我。

en.content:
I bought a yearly gym membership. The monthly auto-debit has gone through eight times.
The card is still in my wallet, condition: brand new.

The front-desk staff looked genuinely shocked when I walked in, and asked if I was here to cancel. I said no, I just came to feel the moment the monthly fee leaves my account.
en.editorNote: Send to the family chat the morning the auto-debit alert wakes you up, ideally with a screenshot of your gym app
variations: Upgrade: I've decided this isn't an exercise fee. It's therapy billed under 'I'm trying'.
Follow-up: at renewal the desk asked if I wanted to switch plans. I said no — keep it the same, it suits me better.

items.sourceNote: 「calories are creatures that sew your clothes tighter at night」概念重寫，加入台灣換季衣櫃情境

zh-tw.content:
我終於知道為什麼牛仔褲越來越緊了——
卡路里其實是一群住在我衣櫃裡的小精靈，
每天晚上趁我睡覺的時候，把我的衣服一件一件偷偷改小。

他們手很巧，連我去年買的那件寬鬆 T 恤都沒放過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合換季整理衣櫃時傳給室友，配一張「這件以前明明很鬆」的試穿照
variations: 進階版：我懷疑他們最近升級了，連褲頭鬆緊帶都不放過。
後續：我打算下次買衣服直接買大兩號，當作預付給精靈的勞務費。

en.content:
I finally figured out why my jeans keep getting tighter.
Calories are actually tiny creatures living in my closet,
and every night while I'm asleep, they go through my wardrobe and take everything in by half an inch.

They're skilled — even the loose T-shirt I bought last year didn't escape.
en.editorNote: Send to your roommate while doing a seasonal closet purge, ideally with a 'this used to be loose' fit check
variations: Upgrade: I think they've leveled up — even the elastic waistbands are getting attention now.
Follow-up: next shopping trip I'm buying two sizes up, basically prepaying the closet creatures for their labor.

items.sourceNote: 「weight loss starts in your head with a decision」概念延伸，加入台灣便利商店掙扎情境

zh-tw.content:
減肥不是從健身房開始的，是從你站在便利商店冰櫃前那一刻開始的。

你伸手拿無糖豆漿，再放回去拿巧克力牛奶，再放回去拿無糖豆漿——
那 30 秒的內心戰爭，就是整個減肥計畫的縮影。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在晚上 10 點便利商店宵夜時間傳給自己看，當作一種儀式
variations: 進階版：今天我贏了，下週可能會輸，但這場仗會一直打下去。
後續：店員看我猶豫太久，默默把巧克力牛奶往前推了一點。

en.content:
Weight loss doesn't start at the gym. It starts the moment you're standing in front of the convenience store fridge.

You reach for the unsweetened soy milk, put it back, grab the chocolate milk, put it back, grab the soy milk again —
those 30 seconds of internal warfare are basically your entire diet plan in miniature.
en.editorNote: Send to yourself at 10pm during the midnight-snack run — treat it like a small ritual
variations: Upgrade: today I won. Next week I might lose. But this fight is ongoing.
Follow-up: the clerk watched me hesitate so long they quietly nudged the chocolate milk forward.

items.sourceNote: 「my favorite exercise is lunch — a cross between a lunge and a crunch」雙關概念，改為台灣下午茶情境

zh-tw.content:
我最常做的運動叫做「午茶深蹲」。
動作分解：彎腰打開蛋糕盒、起身拿叉子、坐下、再起身去拿第二塊。

一組四下，一天三組，三餐後外加宵夜場，年資已滿三年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下午三點精神不濟時傳到辦公室群組，配一張誰買的鳳梨酥
variations: 進階版：教練看到我做這套動作，說我核心穩定度其實很好。
後續：我打算下個月把這套動作申請成新的健身派別，名字就叫「茶蹲」。

en.content:
My most consistent workout is called the 'tea-time squat'.
Movement breakdown: bend to open the cake box, stand to grab a fork, sit down, stand back up for the second slice.

Four reps a set, three sets a day, plus a midnight encore. I have three years of seniority in this discipline.
en.editorNote: Send to the office group chat at 3pm energy crash, ideally with 'who brought the pineapple cakes' as a photo
variations: Upgrade: my coach saw the form and said my core stability is actually pretty solid.
Follow-up: I'm filing the paperwork next month to register this as a new fitness discipline. Working title: tea-squat.

items.sourceNote: 「motivation gets you started, habit keeps you going」概念重寫為台灣減肥日常版

zh-tw.content:
動力是你週日晚上立下的志向，習慣是你週三早上六點還是起得來。

動力會在你看到便當的那一秒消失，習慣會幫你自動把白飯換成半碗。

減肥的真相是：動力是煙火，習慣是路燈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在書桌上、辦公桌前，或健身房置物櫃內側，給未來那個想偷懶的自己看
variations: 進階版：第一週靠動力，第二個月靠習慣，第三年靠的是「不想再從頭來一次」。
後續：今天我沒運動，但我喝了水、睡飽了，習慣的路燈還亮著。

en.content:
Motivation is the goal you set on Sunday night. Habit is the reason you're still up at 6am on Wednesday.

Motivation disappears the second you see takeout. Habit quietly swaps your full rice bowl for a half portion.

The real truth about weight loss: motivation is fireworks, habit is the streetlight.
en.editorNote: Stick it on your desk, your office monitor, or the inside of your gym locker — for the future you who wants to skip a day
variations: Upgrade: week one runs on motivation. Month two runs on habit. Year three runs on 'I refuse to start over again'.
Follow-up: I didn't work out today. But I drank water and slept enough. The habit streetlight is still on.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't do squats. My relationship with gravity is already complicated」原典重寫，加台灣捷運博愛座場景

zh-tw.content:
教練問我為什麼不做深蹲。
我說：我跟地心引力的關係已經夠複雜了，不需要再每天蹲下去確認一次它還愛我。

證據就是——我每次坐下，地球都用比平常多 0.3 秒的時間才把我接住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次練腿日就裝病的朋友——他會回你「太懂我了」
variations: 後續：今天我坐捷運博愛座，旁邊的阿伯說「年輕人坐得很穩喔」——他不知道我是站不起來。
進階版：我已經把深蹲外包給社區的樓梯間，每天上下五樓，這叫做被動式重訓。

en.content:
My trainer asked why I skip squats.
I told him: my relationship with gravity is already complicated. I don't need to crouch down every day to confirm it still loves me.

The evidence: every time I sit down, the earth takes an extra 0.3 seconds longer than usual to catch me.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who fakes an injury every leg day — they'll reply 'finally, someone gets it'
variations: Follow-up: today an old man on the subway told me 'you sit very steadily for a young person'. He didn't know it's because I can't stand back up.
Pro version: I've outsourced my squats to the apartment stairwell — five floors up and down daily. I call it passive strength training.

items.sourceNote: 「I'd like to thank my personal trainer for the muscles. And my pizza delivery guy for the motivation」原典重寫，加台灣外送員場景

zh-tw.content:
我想感謝兩個人：
感謝教練，給了我肌肉。
感謝外送員，給了我去健身房的動力。

沒有他每週三次準時送到的鹹酥雞，我永遠不會知道自己原來需要運動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合健身完打卡發限動，配一張運動鞋跟外送收據的併排照
variations: 更新：今天我順便感謝便利商店店員，他賣給我的微波義大利麵，讓我下定決心明天一定要去健身房。
進階版：我已經把外送員列入我的健身夥伴名單——他是我唯一持續超過半年的健身夥伴。

en.content:
I'd like to thank two people:
Thanks to my trainer for the muscles.
Thanks to my delivery driver for the motivation.

Without the fried chicken arriving at my door three times a week, I would never have realized I needed to work out.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a post-workout check-in story — pair with a side-by-side of your trainers and the delivery receipt
variations: Update: today I also want to thank the convenience store clerk. The microwave pasta they sold me convinced me to go to the gym tomorrow.
Pro tier: I've officially added my delivery driver to my fitness buddy list — he's the only fitness buddy I've kept for more than six months.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't need to be perfect to be worthy. I just need to show up」概念重寫為溫柔減肥版，加台灣日常場景

zh-tw.content:
你不需要完美才值得瘦下來。
你今天少點了一杯手搖、樓梯走了多兩層、宵夜換成水煮蛋——

這些不會立刻變成腹肌，但它們會慢慢長成一個更喜歡自己的你。

減肥不是把現在的你丟掉，是讓未來的你多一個選項。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合凌晨低潮時傳給自己，或健身房戰友——這一句不需要圖，直接放著就好
variations: 進階版：減肥的終點不是體重計上的數字，是某天早上你穿衣服時，不再對著鏡子嘆氣。
後續：今天我只多走了一站捷運，但我為這一站感到驕傲——這就夠了。

en.content:
You don't need to be perfect to deserve a smaller version of yourself.
Today you skipped the bubble tea, took the stairs two flights, swapped your midnight ramen for a boiled egg —

these things won't turn into abs by tomorrow, but they'll slowly grow into a version of you who likes yourself a little more.

Dieting isn't about throwing away who you are now. It's about giving future-you one more option.
en.editorNote: Save for a late-night low moment, or send to a gym buddy — this one doesn't need a photo, just let it sit
variations: Pro version: the finish line of dieting isn't the number on the scale. It's a morning when you put on clothes and don't sigh at the mirror.
Follow-up: today I walked one extra subway stop. I'm proud of that one stop. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: 夏天前最後一週減肥衝刺的自嘲，原創語錄；台灣端午粽子在地化

zh-tw.content:
夏天剩兩週，端午節剩四天。
我的減肥計畫遇到了人生中最強對手——肉粽。

它躺在電鍋裡，散發著一種「你已經撐到五月了，沒差這幾顆」的氣息。
我看著它，它看著我，我們達成共識：先過完端午再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每年端午都重新立志的朋友——他會邊吃粽子邊回你「我懂」
variations: 更新：吃完三顆肉粽後，我決定把減肥計畫延到中秋——畢竟夏天還很長。
進階版：今年我發明了「節日減肥法」——所有節日都先過完，剩下的日子認真執行，結果一年只剩 47 天。

en.content:
Two weeks until summer. Four days until Dragon Boat Festival.
My diet plan just met its toughest opponent in life — sticky rice dumplings.

They sit in the rice cooker, radiating an energy that says, 'you've made it to May, what's a few more dumplings going to do?'
I look at them, they look at me. We reach an agreement: let's get through the festival first.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who renews their diet vows every holiday — they'll reply mid-dumpling, 'I get it'
variations: Update: after three dumplings, I've officially postponed the diet to Mid-Autumn Festival. Summer's still long.
Pro version: this year I invented the 'holiday-first diet' — finish every holiday first, then get serious. Turns out the year only has 47 actual diet days.

items.sourceNote: 「the only time to eat diet food is while you're waiting for the steak to cook」經典格式重寫，加台灣牛排館場景

zh-tw.content:
減肥餐唯一適合的時機，是你在等牛排上桌的那十分鐘。
那段時間裡，你可以盡情吃沙拉吧、配玉米濃湯、外加兩塊蒜香麵包。

等牛排來了，沙拉只是前菜——這是邏輯，不是放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次去吃牛排都先把沙拉吧掃過三輪的朋友——他會回你「這叫前情提要」
variations: 升級版：沙拉吧再去第四輪的時候，店員看我的眼神已經從尊敬變成了同情。
後續：服務生問我牛排要幾分熟，我說「等我吃完冰淇淋再決定」。

en.content:
The only appropriate time to eat diet food is during the ten minutes you're waiting for your steak.
In that window, you can demolish the salad bar, knock back the corn chowder, and grab two cloves of garlic bread.

When the steak arrives, the salad becomes the appetizer. This is logic, not surrender.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sweeps the salad bar three times before the entrée arrives — they'll reply 'I call this the prologue'
variations: Upgrade: by the fourth trip to the salad bar, the staff's expression shifts from respect to genuine concern.
Follow-up: the server asked how I wanted the steak cooked. I said 'check back after I finish dessert'.

items.sourceNote: 「該減的不是肥，是慾望」毒雞湯減肥金句反向重寫，加自嘲口吻

zh-tw.content:
別人說：「該減的不是肥，是慾望。」
我認真想了三秒——

如果我能減掉慾望，那我早就不會半夜兩點站在 7-11 的關東煮鍋前面，認真挑高麗菜捲、米血糕、跟兩根熱狗了。

慾望是肥的源頭。但慾望也是我活著的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次看到減肥金句都自動翻譯成宵夜選單的朋友——他會立刻轉發給超商店員
variations: 後續：今晚我嘗試只買一杯無糖綠茶，結果順手加購了兩顆茶葉蛋——這叫做意志力的局部勝利。
毒雞湯升級版：你不是胖，你只是把人生過得很豐盛——這聽起來比較不傷自尊。

en.content:
Other people say, "What you really need to cut isn't fat — it's desire."
I thought about it for three seconds.

If I could cut my desire, I wouldn't be standing in front of the 7-Eleven hot food counter at 2 a.m., carefully choosing between cabbage rolls, pig's blood cake, and two hot dogs.

Desire is the source of fat. But desire is also proof I'm still alive.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who auto-translates every diet quote into a midnight snack menu — they'll forward it straight to the cashier
variations: Follow-up: tonight I tried to only buy unsweetened green tea. I ended up adding two tea eggs at checkout. Call it a partial victory of willpower.
Toxic motivation upgrade: you're not fat, you've just lived life generously. Sounds way easier on the ego.

items.sourceNote: 「the first thing you lose on a diet is your sense of humor」原典重寫，加台灣同事辦公室在地場景

zh-tw.content:
減肥第一個瘦下來的，永遠不是肚子，是脾氣。

第三天我同事跟我借便利貼，我盯著他看了七秒，差點哭出來。
第五天主管說早安，我回他「祝你今天少吃一點」。
第七天我已經被全辦公室列入「最好不要在午餐前找他講話」的名單。

體重沒掉，人緣先掉了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次節食就變得很兇的朋友——他會立刻回你「我同事剛才也閃過」
variations: 升級版：減肥第十天，我已經練成「用眼神拒絕同事的下午茶」的超能力。
後續：今天我終於崩潰，吃了同事帶來的鳳梨酥，全辦公室爆出歡呼——他們以為我終於走出來了。

en.content:
The first thing to shrink on a diet is never your waistline. It's your patience.

Day three, a coworker asked to borrow a sticky note. I stared at him for seven seconds and almost cried.
Day five, my manager said good morning. I replied, "hope you eat less today."
Day seven, the whole office has me on a list labeled 'do not approach before lunch'.

The weight hasn't budged. My reputation already has.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who turns feral every time they start a diet — they'll reply 'a coworker just dodged me'
variations: Day ten upgrade: I've now mastered the power of declining the office afternoon tea cart with a single glare.
Follow-up: I finally cracked today and ate a coworker's pineapple cake. The entire office cheered — they thought I'd escaped a cult.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm allergic to mornings」格式重寫，加台灣晨跑場景的自嘲

zh-tw.content:
我一直想當那種「早上六點起來跑步」的人。
但每次鬧鐘響起來，我都會誠實地問自己一個問題——

「你瘦了會比較開心，還是再睡兩小時比較開心？」

這個問題我已經回答了九年，答案從來沒變過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都把鬧鐘設好但永遠按掉的朋友——他會回你「我剛才又輸了」
variations: 升級版：今天我終於六點起床了，但只是為了去買早餐店剛開的蛋餅——這也算贏。
後續：我發現晨跑的人都很安靜，因為他們的對手是自己；而我的對手是被窩，比較難贏。

en.content:
I've always wanted to be the kind of person who runs at 6 a.m.
But every time the alarm goes off, I ask myself one honest question —

"will you be happier thinner, or happier with two more hours of sleep?"

I've been answering this question for nine years. The answer has never changed.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sets six alarms and snoozes all of them — they'll reply 'I lost again just now'
variations: Upgrade: today I actually got up at 6, but only to grab the first breakfast crepe of the day. Still counts as a win.
Follow-up: morning runners are quiet because their opponent is themselves. My opponent is the duvet — much harder to beat.

items.sourceNote: 夏天前最後衝刺與夏季短褲焦慮格式，原創語錄；台灣熱浪在地化

zh-tw.content:
天氣預報說：明天三十四度，未來一週都是夏天了。
我打開衣櫃，去年的短褲認真地看著我。

我說：「我們今年再相處看看。」
短褲說：「沒事，你站著穿可以，坐下就不行。」

人類跟衣服的關係，比任何感情都要誠實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合五月底打開衣櫃翻夏裝那一刻發限動，配一張穿不下短褲的腿部特寫
variations: 更新：今天我成功扣上短褲扣子，但坐下的瞬間，扣子彈出去打到電視——它選擇了自由。
後續：我決定今年夏天就走「飄逸長裙風」，這叫做穿搭策略，不叫逃避。

en.content:
The weather app says: 34°C tomorrow, summer for the rest of the week.
I open the closet. Last year's shorts stare at me.

I say, "let's try this one more year."
The shorts say, "standing up is fine. Sitting down is not."

The relationship between humans and clothes is more honest than any relationship I've ever had.
en.editorNote: Perfect for the late-May moment you finally open the summer drawer, paired with a close-up of shorts that refuse to button
variations: Update: today I got the button closed. The second I sat down, the button shot off and hit the TV. It chose freedom.
Follow-up: I've decided this summer is a 'flowy long skirts' summer. This is called a styling strategy, not an escape plan.

items.sourceNote: 間歇性斷食「進食窗口」變得失控的格式重寫，加台灣便利商店與宵夜在地化

zh-tw.content:
我開始做間歇性斷食，理論上只能在一個八小時的「進食窗口」裡吃東西。
問題是，我的窗口從中午開到凌晨，中間還跨了一趟便利商店、兩攤鹹酥雞。

醫生問我窗口幾點關，我說：「等我睡著它就會自己關了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給嘴上說在斷食、實際從早吃到晚的朋友——他會回你「我的窗口是落地窗」
variations: 進階版：我的進食窗口最近升級成「進食落地窗」，再不控制就要變成「進食整面玻璃帷幕」了。
後續：我問營養師窗口可不可以分期付款，她沉默了很久。

en.content:
I started intermittent fasting. In theory I can only eat inside one eight-hour 'eating window'.
The problem is, my window opens at noon and doesn't close until 2 a.m., with a convenience store run and two fried-chicken stalls in between.

The doctor asked when my window closes. I said, 'it closes by itself once I fall asleep.'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who claims to be fasting but eats from morning to midnight — they'll reply 'my window is a sliding glass door'
variations: Pro version: my eating window has been upgraded to an eating patio door. If I don't get it under control it's becoming a full glass curtain wall.
Follow-up: I asked my nutritionist if the window could be paid off in installments. She went quiet for a long time.

items.sourceNote: 「protein shake tastes like optimism blended with chalk」格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲對照在地化

zh-tw.content:
我認真買了一大罐乳清蛋白，說要替代手搖飲。
第一口下去，我終於懂了——這個味道，是把「上進心」跟「石膏粉」一起打成漿。

喝完第三天，我獎勵自己一杯珍奶半糖去冰，喝的當下我哭了，不是因為糖，是因為終於有味道。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次說要戒手搖、結果買了高蛋白還是偷喝的朋友——他會立刻認同這個味道
variations: 進階版：我把高蛋白加進珍奶裡，想說兩全其美，結果它同時失去了健康跟好喝。
後續：那罐高蛋白現在被我拿來當啞鈴，至少這樣它有在幫我減肥。

en.content:
I committed to a giant tub of whey protein to replace my bubble tea habit.
First sip and I finally understood — this flavor is what happens when you blend ambition with powdered chalk.

On day three I rewarded myself with a half-sugar bubble tea, and I cried mid-sip. Not because of the sugar. Because it finally had a taste.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps quitting bubble tea, buys protein powder, and sneaks the tea anyway — they'll vouch for the flavor
variations: Pro version: I added the protein powder to my bubble tea for the best of both worlds. It instantly lost both the health and the taste.
Follow-up: that protein tub is now my dumbbell. At least this way it's actually helping me lose weight.

items.sourceNote: 「I ordered salad, then remembered who I am and upgraded to fries」格式重寫，加台灣自助餐／便當在地化

zh-tw.content:
我走進自助餐，立志今天只夾燙青菜跟一塊雞胸。
夾到第三樣的時候，我突然想起了我是誰——

於是我的餐盤上多了滷排骨、半熟蛋、爌肉，還有一勺淋了滿滿肉燥的白飯。

青菜還在，它孤單地躺在角落，像個提醒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次進自助餐都「只夾青菜」結果端出一座肉山的朋友——他會回你「這是我今天的午餐」
variations: 進階版：阿姨幫我打菜的時候多舀了一勺肉燥，我沒有阻止——這不是我的錯，這是台灣人的禮貌。
後續：結帳的時候老闆看了一眼我的盤子，默默把「健康餐」的牌子收起來了。

en.content:
I walked into the cafeteria determined to grab only steamed greens and one piece of chicken breast.
By the third item, I remembered who I am —

and my tray suddenly held braised pork ribs, a soft-boiled egg, stewed pork belly, and a scoop of rice drowning in meat sauce.

The greens are still there, lying alone in the corner like a warning.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who enters the buffet 'for just vegetables' and leaves with a mountain of meat — they'll reply 'that's literally my lunch today'
variations: Pro version: the server added an extra scoop of meat sauce and I didn't stop her. This isn't my fault. This is Taiwanese politeness.
Follow-up: at checkout the owner glanced at my tray and quietly took down the 'healthy meal' sign.

items.sourceNote: 「My cheat day is every day that ends with y」格式重寫，加台灣作弊日／放縱日在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的減肥計畫有一個彈性很大的「作弊日」。
原本設定一週一天，後來變成週末兩天，再後來凡是名字裡有「一二三四五六日」的日子，都可以作弊。

我攤開行事曆認真研究，發現一週七天剛好全部符合條件。
所以嚴格來說，我每天都很守規矩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給「作弊日比減肥日還多」的朋友——他會回你「那我們根本天天合格」
variations: 進階版：我後來把作弊日改成「獎勵日」，聽起來比較正向，效果一模一樣。
後續：朋友問我哪天是減肥日，我認真想了三分鐘，回答：「應該是去年某一天。」

en.content:
My diet plan has a very flexible 'cheat day'.
It started as one day a week, then became both weekend days, then any day with a name ending in 'y'.

I laid out the calendar and studied it carefully. All seven days of the week qualify.
So technically, I follow the rules every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cheat days outnumber their diet days — they'll reply 'so we both pass every day'
variations: Pro version: I later renamed it 'reward day' to sound more positive. Same effect, better branding.
Follow-up: a friend asked which day was my diet day. I thought hard for three minutes and answered, 'probably some day last year.'

items.sourceNote: 「Me: I want abs. Also me: I want pizza」雙重內心戲格式重寫，加台灣鹹酥雞／滷味在地化

zh-tw.content:
我：我想要馬甲線。
也是我：我想要鹹酥雞加雞排加滷味大全配。

我：我想要在夏天穿得好看。
也是我：我想要在沙發上躺成一隻吐司。

這兩個我每天都在開會，但從來沒有人退讓，最後通常是鹹酥雞那邊獲勝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都在跟自己內心拔河的朋友——他會回你「今天又是鹹酥雞贏了」
variations: 更新：今天兩個我終於和解了，協議內容是「先吃鹹酥雞，馬甲線明年再說」。
後續：我發現想要馬甲線的那個我，通常只在剛洗完澡照鏡子的那三分鐘出現。

en.content:
Me: I want abs.
Also me: I want fried chicken, a chicken cutlet, and the full braised-snack platter.

Me: I want to look good this summer.
Also me: I want to lie on the couch flattened like a slice of toast.

These two versions of me meet every day. Neither ever backs down. The fried chicken side usually wins.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a daily tug-of-war with themselves — they'll reply 'fried chicken won again today'
variations: Update: the two of me finally reached a settlement today. The terms: eat the fried chicken now, revisit the abs next year.
Follow-up: I've noticed the version of me that wants abs only shows up for the three minutes I'm looking in the mirror after a shower.

items.sourceNote: 「I stepped on the scale and it asked for my username and password」格式重寫，加台灣體重計在地化

zh-tw.content:
今天早上我踏上體重計。
它沉默了一下，螢幕跳出一行字：「請先登入帳號密碼，並升級為旗艦會員。」

我退後一步，它又補了一句：「另建議綁定信用卡，方便後續分期。」

從那天起，我跟體重計變成了一種，很有距離感的關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天早上量體重就心情低落的朋友——他會回你「我已經一個月沒登入了」
variations: 後續：我換了一台新的體重計，這台比較貼心，量完直接顯示「資料維護中，請稍後再試」——它選擇保護我的心情。
進階版：我決定改用「目測法」量體重，誤差很大，但心情非常穩定。

en.content:
I stepped on the scale this morning.
It went quiet for a second, then displayed a line of text: 'Please log in. Premium membership required for full access.'

I stepped back. It added: 'We also recommend linking a credit card for future installment plans.'

From that day on, the scale and I have kept a respectful distance.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose mood crashes after the morning weigh-in — they'll reply 'I haven't logged in for a month'
variations: Follow-up: I bought a new scale. This one is more considerate — it just shows 'data under maintenance, try again later'. It chose to protect my feelings.
Pro version: I've switched to estimating my weight by eye. The margin of error is huge, but my mood is very stable.

items.sourceNote: 「My warm-up is longer than my workout, I'm just here for the snacks」格式重寫，加台灣健身房在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的熱身時間，比我正式運動的時間還長。
滑手機十分鐘、調整一下髮帶、拍兩張限動、研究器材說明書、跟櫃檯閒聊——

等我真正準備好的時候，剛好到了該去買蛋白飲的時間。

所以嚴格來說，我每次都有來健身房，只是沒怎麼健身。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次去健身房都拍照拍最久的朋友——他會回你「重點是儀式感」
variations: 進階版：我把『拍限動』算進有氧訓練，畢竟舉手機那個動作，做久了肩膀也會痠。
後續：教練問我今天練了什麼，我認真回答『主要練了等待』——等器材、等心情、等下班。

en.content:
My warm-up takes longer than my actual workout.
Ten minutes scrolling my phone, adjusting my headband, posting two stories, reading the equipment manual, chatting with the front desk —

by the time I'm truly ready, it's conveniently time to go buy a protein shake.

So technically, I show up to the gym every time. I just don't do much gym.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spends the most time taking photos at the gym — they'll reply 'it's about the ritual'
variations: Pro version: I count 'posting stories' as cardio. Holding the phone up that long, your shoulders genuinely start to ache.
Follow-up: my trainer asked what I worked on today. I honestly answered, 'mostly waiting' — for the machine, for the mood, for closing time.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't skip leg day, I skip every day that involves legs」格式重寫，加台灣健身房在地化

zh-tw.content:
有人問我會不會偷懶不練腿日。
我說怎麼會，我超有原則的——

我不是只跳過腿日，我是公平地跳過每一個需要用到腿的日子。

胸日、背日、肩日，只要它名字裡有『日』，我一律一視同仁地請假。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都說『今天剛好休息日』的健身房朋友——他會回你『我們是同一種人』
variations: 進階版：我最認真執行的訓練是『臥推』——平躺在沙發上，把遙控器推來推去。
後續：教練幫我排了一週六練，我看完課表，默默把它收進『勵志收藏』資料夾，再也沒打開。

en.content:
Someone asked if I skip leg day.
I said, never — I have strong principles.

I don't just skip leg day. I fairly skip every single day that involves legs.

Chest day, back day, shoulder day — as long as its name ends in 'day', I take a uniform, impartial absence.
en.editorNote: Send to the gym friend who always says 'today's conveniently a rest day' — they'll reply 'we're the same species'
variations: Pro version: the exercise I'm most committed to is the bench press — lying flat on the couch, pressing the remote back and forth.
Follow-up: my trainer built me a six-day-a-week plan. I read it once, quietly filed it under 'inspirational saves', and never opened it again.

items.sourceNote: 「I think about food 90% of the time」格式重寫，加台灣美食內心戲在地化

zh-tw.content:
別人看我安靜地坐在那邊，以為我在思考人生大事。

其實我腦中正在進行一場激烈的辯論：
晚餐到底要吃麻辣鍋、鹹酥雞，還是乾脆兩個都來。

我清醒的時間裡，大概有九成都在想等一下要吃什麼——
剩下那一成，是在想剛剛那餐有沒有吃夠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給開會放空時都在想晚餐的朋友——他會回你『被你說中了』
variations: 進階版：我的大腦有一個專門負責規劃三餐的部門，運轉效率比我工作時高出十倍。
後續：朋友問我在想什麼，我說『沒事』，其實我正在心裡比較兩家便當店的滷蛋大小。

en.content:
People see me sitting there quietly and assume I'm pondering life's big questions.

In reality, an intense debate is raging in my head:
should dinner be hot pot, fried chicken, or honestly, just both.

Roughly ninety percent of my waking hours go to thinking about what to eat next —
the remaining ten percent is spent wondering if the last meal was enough.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who zones out in meetings planning dinner — they'll reply 'you called me out'
variations: Pro version: my brain has a dedicated department for meal planning. It runs ten times more efficiently than I do at work.
Follow-up: a friend asked what I was thinking about. I said 'nothing'. I was actually comparing the braised-egg sizes at two different lunch box shops.

items.sourceNote: 「Burpees? I thought you said slurpees」諧音梗格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練在課表上寫了『波比跳三組』。
我看成『波霸奶三杯』，立刻覺得這堂課好有誠意。

等我興高采烈地問他要不要去糖，他沉默了五秒，
說：『同學，那是運動，不是點餐。』

從此我對健身的熱情，下降了大概半糖的程度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次看到運動名稱都先聯想到食物的朋友——他會回你『所以你也是』
variations: 進階版：後來我發現『深蹲』唸快一點很像『甜甜』，於是我每蹲一下就更想吃甜點，這堂課完全失控。
後續：教練最後妥協了，他說只要我認真做完，下課可以一起去買手搖——這是我運動史上最強的動力來源。

en.content:
My trainer wrote 'three sets of burpees' on the workout plan.
I read it as 'three cups of boba', and suddenly the class felt full of heart.

When I cheerfully asked him whether I should get them less-sweet, he went silent for five seconds and said,
'That's an exercise, not a drink order.'

My enthusiasm for fitness dropped by roughly half-sugar that day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who hears any exercise name and immediately thinks of food — they'll reply 'so you're one of us too'
variations: Pro version: I later realized 'squats' sounds a bit like 'snacks' if you say it fast, so every rep made me crave dessert more. The class went fully off the rails.
Follow-up: my trainer finally caved and said if I finished the set seriously, we could grab bubble tea after. Strongest source of motivation in my entire fitness history.

items.sourceNote: 「one salad and one workout later, expecting miracles」夏日身材落差格式重寫，加台灣夏天場景

zh-tw.content:
我今天吃了一份沙拉，又做了二十下深蹲。
做完立刻轉身照鏡子，期待看到全新的自己。

鏡子裡的人跟早上一模一樣，只是現在比較喘。

夏天剩不到一個月，但我的身材顯然還活在去年的冬天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次運動完就照鏡子等奇蹟的朋友——他會回你『所以為什麼還沒瘦』
variations: 進階版：我把那份沙拉的照片存起來，準備夏天結束後拿出來說『我有努力過』。
後續：第二天我又吃了一份沙拉，這次連深蹲都省了，反正鏡子也不會給面子。

en.content:
Today I ate one salad and did twenty squats.
Then I spun around to the mirror, expecting to meet a brand-new me.

The person in the mirror looked exactly like this morning, just slightly more out of breath.

Less than a month until summer, and my body is clearly still living in last winter.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who checks the mirror for miracles right after a workout — they'll reply 'so why am I not thin yet'
variations: Pro version: I saved a photo of that salad to pull out after summer and say 'I did try.'
Follow-up: day two I ate another salad and skipped the squats entirely — the mirror wasn't going to cut me any slack anyway.

items.sourceNote: 「the scale asked for my username and password」體重計擬人化格式重寫，加台灣場景

zh-tw.content:
我早上站上體重計，它沉默了好久，
然後緩緩跳出一行字：「請輸入帳號密碼。」

我不知道哪來的資料量，需要登入才看得完。

後來我們達成共識：以後我不站，它也不顯示，彼此留點尊嚴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天早上跟體重計鬥智的朋友——他會回你『我們也分手了』
variations: 進階版：第二天它直接顯示『系統維護中』，我知道它只是不想面對現實。
後續：我把體重計收進櫃子，它在裡面震了一下——那是如釋重負。

en.content:
I stepped on the scale this morning. It went silent for a long time,
then slowly displayed: 'please enter your username and password.'

I don't know what data volume requires a login to view in full.

We later reached an agreement: I don't step on, it doesn't display, and we both keep our dignity.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend locked in a daily standoff with their scale — they'll reply 'we broke up too'
variations: Pro version: day two it just showed 'system under maintenance'. I knew it simply didn't want to face reality.
Follow-up: I put the scale away in the cabinet. It buzzed once inside — that was relief.

items.sourceNote: 「I do squats so my snacks have a nice shelf」健身房自嘲格式重寫，加台灣零食場景

zh-tw.content:
有人問我為什麼這麼認真練深蹲。
我說：因為這樣我的肚子才有一個穩固的層架，
可以放鹹酥雞、放手搖飲、放整袋洋芋片。

練的不是身材，是收納——這叫做機能性訓練。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次運動完都要配零食慶祝的朋友——他會說『原來我一直在練收納』
variations: 進階版：今天我加練核心，是為了讓那個層架更穩，珍奶放上去才不會晃。
後續：朋友看完說想跟我一起練，我說好，下課我們先去買零食量一下尺寸。

en.content:
Someone asked why I take squats so seriously.
I said: so my stomach has a sturdy shelf
to hold fried chicken, bubble tea, and a whole bag of chips.

I'm not training my body. I'm training storage. Call it functional fitness.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who celebrates every workout with snacks — they'll say 'turns out I've been training storage all along'
variations: Pro version: today I added core work to make the shelf more stable, so the bubble tea doesn't wobble when I set it down.
Follow-up: a friend said they want to train with me. I said sure — after class we'll buy snacks first to measure the dimensions.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm not addicted to sugar, we're in a committed relationship」食物擬人戀愛格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲場景

zh-tw.content:
別人說我對糖上癮，我覺得這個說法太不尊重了。
我跟糖不是上癮的關係，我們是穩定交往的伴侶。

每天下午三點，手搖飲準時報到，從不放我鴿子。
你說，這種對象去哪裡找？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天下午都要一杯手搖才能活的朋友——他會回你『這就是真愛』
variations: 進階版：我們最近還決定同居了，現在冰箱裡長期備著珍奶跟布丁——這叫做交往的承諾。
後續：朋友勸我分手，我說可以啊，但要等他先幫我找到一個一樣準時又一樣甜的對象。

en.content:
People say I'm addicted to sugar. I find that deeply disrespectful.
Sugar and I aren't an addiction. We're a committed couple in a stable relationship.

Every day at three p.m., bubble tea shows up on time and never stands me up.
Tell me, where else would I find a partner like that?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't function without an afternoon bubble tea — they'll reply 'this is true love'
variations: Pro version: we recently decided to move in together. The fridge is now permanently stocked with bubble tea and pudding — that's commitment.
Follow-up: a friend told me to break up. I said sure, right after they find me someone equally punctual and equally sweet.

items.sourceNote: 「my diet starts tomorrow, it's been starting tomorrow for six months」永恆延後格式重寫，加台灣行事曆在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的減肥計畫，明天開始。
這個「明天」已經連續開始了半年，從來沒有真正抵達過。

我懷疑「明天」其實是個地名，而且離我家很遠——
遠到我每次出發前，都會先吃個東西補充體力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都說「明天開始」的朋友——他會回你「我明天真的會開始」
variations: 進階版：科學家說明天永遠不會來，這是哲學；對我來說這是免死金牌。
後續：我把「明天」設成手機鬧鐘，結果它每天響的時候，我都按了貪睡。

en.content:
My diet starts tomorrow.
This 'tomorrow' has been starting for six months straight and has never once actually arrived.

I'm starting to suspect 'tomorrow' is a place, and it's very far from my house —
so far that I eat something first, every time, to fuel up for the trip.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'starting tomorrow' daily — they'll reply 'I'll really start tomorrow'
variations: Advanced version: scientists say tomorrow never comes. For philosophers that's a paradox. For me it's a get-out-of-jail-free card.
Follow-up: I set 'tomorrow' as a phone alarm. Every day it goes off, I hit snooze.

items.sourceNote: 「I ordered salad, then remembered who I am and upgraded to fries」格式重寫，加台灣點餐在地化

zh-tw.content:
我點餐的時候，先勇敢地說：「我要一份沙拉。」
說完那三秒，我突然想起我是誰，
於是改口：「不好意思，沙拉取消，換成炸雞便當加薯條。」

做自己，是減肥路上最大的障礙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次點餐都在沙拉跟便當之間搖擺的朋友——他會回你「我也是這樣輸的」
variations: 進階版：店員問我確定嗎，我說確定，畢竟我這個人最大的優點就是誠實面對食慾。
後續：隔壁桌的人點了沙拉，我看著他，心想——他大概還沒想起自己是誰。

en.content:
When I order, I bravely say: 'I'll have a salad.'
For about three seconds I feel proud. Then I remember who I am,
so I correct myself: 'Actually, cancel the salad — make it the fried chicken box with fries.'

Being true to yourself is the single biggest obstacle on a diet.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who wavers between salad and a rice box every time — they'll reply 'this is exactly how I lose too'
variations: Advanced version: the cashier asked if I was sure. I said yes — my best quality is being honest about my appetite.
Follow-up: someone at the next table ordered a salad. I looked at them and thought — they probably haven't remembered who they are yet.

items.sourceNote: 「the scale and I have a complicated relationship, sometimes I block its number」體重計擬人格式重寫，加在地化

zh-tw.content:
我跟體重計的關係非常複雜。
我們每週見一次，有時候會擁抱，有時候我會直接把它封鎖。

上禮拜它說了一句我不愛聽的話，
我把它收進櫃子最深處，冷戰了整整七天——這叫做關係的界線。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次站上體重計都要先深呼吸的朋友——他會回你「我已經三週沒理它了」
variations: 進階版：和好的方式很簡單，只要它哪天願意往下掉個零點五公斤，我立刻原諒它。
後續：我考慮換一台會說好聽話的體重計，結果發現那種叫做「鏡子騙局」，要另外付費。

en.content:
The scale and I have a very complicated relationship.
We see each other once a week. Sometimes we hug. Sometimes I just block its number.

Last week it said something I didn't want to hear,
so I shoved it to the back of the cupboard and gave it the silent treatment for seven full days. That's called setting boundaries.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who takes a deep breath before stepping on the scale — they'll reply 'I've been ignoring mine for three weeks'
variations: Advanced version: making up is easy. The day it's willing to drop half a kilo, I forgive it instantly.
Follow-up: I considered switching to a scale that says nicer things. Turns out that's just a mirror, and it costs extra.

items.sourceNote: 「my favorite exercise at the gym would probably be judging」健身房旁觀自嘲格式重寫，加台灣健身房場景

zh-tw.content:
有人問我在健身房最常做的運動是什麼。
我認真想了想，回答：觀察別人。

我可以坐在拉筋區整整四十分鐘，研究每個人的姿勢、配色、喝什麼蛋白粉——
唯一沒研究的，就是我自己到底有沒有動到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給辦了健身房會員但都在滑手機的朋友——他會回你「我也是研究組的」
variations: 進階版：我最近升級了，從觀察別人進步到觀察自己——主要是觀察我在鏡子裡看起來累不累。
後續：教練問我今天練了什麼，我說『核心』——主要是核心地坐著，看了一個半小時。

en.content:
Someone asked what exercise I do most at the gym.
I thought about it seriously and answered: watching other people.

I can sit in the stretching area for a solid forty minutes, studying everyone's form, color coordination, and which protein powder they drink —
the only thing I never study is whether I've moved at all.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend with a gym membership who just scrolls their phone there — they'll reply 'I'm in the observation department too'
variations: Advanced version: I recently leveled up from watching others to watching myself — mainly watching how tired I look in the mirror.
Follow-up: my trainer asked what I trained today. I said 'core' — core as in I sat there at the center of the room for ninety minutes.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't diet, I clean-bulk with pizza」健身術語自我合理化格式重寫，加台灣食物在地化

zh-tw.content:
我從來不減肥，我這叫「乾淨增肌」。
所謂乾淨，就是吃之前先把鹹酥雞的紙巾擦乾淨；
所謂增肌，就是相信這些蛋白質遲早會變成肌肉。

至於它們先變成什麼——那是身體自己的安排，我不便干涉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次吃宵夜都說「在增肌」的健身朋友——他會回你「這是專業術語」
variations: 進階版：今天我吃了三份排骨飯，這叫做『熱量盈餘』，是增肌的必要條件，請不要質疑科學。
後續：教練看了我的飲食紀錄，沉默地把『乾淨』兩個字劃掉，改成『盡力』。

en.content:
I never diet. I call it a 'clean bulk'.
The 'clean' part means I wipe the grease off the fried chicken napkin before eating;
the 'bulk' part means I trust this protein will eventually become muscle.

What it turns into first — that's my body's own arrangement, and I'd rather not interfere.
en.editorNote: Send to the gym friend who calls every midnight snack a 'bulk' — they'll reply 'this is professional terminology'
variations: Advanced version: today I ate three pork chop rice boxes. That's a 'caloric surplus' — a necessary condition for muscle gain. Please don't question the science.
Follow-up: my trainer looked at my food log, silently crossed out 'clean', and wrote 'trying'.

items.sourceNote: 「Leg day? I thought you said keg day」諧音梗重寫，改成中文手搖飲諧音與在地化

zh-tw.content:
朋友揪我去練「腿日」。
我聽成「退一」，以為是手搖飲第二杯半價，立刻說好。

結果到現場才發現，沒有飲料，只有深蹲。
從那天起，我聽到任何運動名稱，都會先確認一次到底有沒有東西可以喝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次被揪健身都先問「結束後吃什麼」的朋友——他會說這叫做動機管理
variations: 後續：隔天我真的去買了第二杯半價的珍奶，慰勞我那雙再也蹲不下去的腿。

en.content:
A friend invited me to leg day.
I heard 'keg day' and immediately said yes, picturing a fridge full of cold drinks.

I showed up to find no drinks. Only squats.
Ever since, whenever someone names a workout, I check first whether there's anything to drink involved.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who answers every gym invite with 'what are we eating after' — they'll call it motivation management
variations: Follow-up: the next day I genuinely bought a celebratory drink to console the legs that could no longer bend.

items.sourceNote: 「my gym membership is basically a very expensive nap pass / energy-saving mode like my smartwatch」格式重寫，改成在地省電模式哏

zh-tw.content:
我不是懶，我只是開了「省電模式」。
螢幕變暗、背景程式關閉、所有非必要的運動一律停止——只保留呼吸跟滑手機這兩個核心功能。

電量回到 100% 之前，請不要叫我去健身房，謝謝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次約運動都說「我先充個電」的朋友——他會回你「我現在 3% 不能動」
variations: 進階版：我已經連續低電量警告三個月，醫生說再不充電會自動關機，我說那叫休息。

en.content:
I'm not lazy. I'm in energy-saving mode.
Screen dimmed, background apps closed, all non-essential exercise suspended — only breathing and scrolling remain as core functions.

Please don't ask me to go to the gym until I'm back to 100%. Thank you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who replies to every workout invite with 'let me charge up first' — they'll say 'I'm at 3%, can't move'
variations: Advanced version: I've been on low-battery warning for three months. The doctor said I'll shut down if I don't recharge. I call that resting.

items.sourceNote: 「90% of the time I'm thinking about what I'm going to eat later」原典重寫，加在地會議與便當情境

zh-tw.content:
開會的時候，我看起來眉頭深鎖、若有所思。
同事以為我在思考第三季的策略方向。

其實我有九成的腦容量，正在認真評估午餐到底要吃排骨便當還是雞腿便當。
剩下那一成，在想晚餐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次開會都在偷想中午吃什麼的同事——他會回你「被你說中了」
variations: 後續：主管問我有什麼想法，我脫口而出『雞腿』，全場安靜了三秒，然後有人說『我也是』。

en.content:
In meetings, I look deep in thought, brow furrowed, contemplative.
Colleagues assume I'm weighing the third-quarter strategy.

In reality, ninety percent of my brain is seriously evaluating whether lunch should be the pork chop box or the chicken leg box.
The other ten percent is thinking about dinner.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who spends every meeting secretly deciding on lunch — they'll reply 'you caught me'
variations: Follow-up: my boss asked if I had any thoughts. I blurted out 'chicken leg'. The room went silent for three seconds, then someone said 'same'.

items.sourceNote: 「Salad is my spirit animal, but it also demands croutons, bacon, and ranch」格式重寫，加台式沙拉配料在地化

zh-tw.content:
沙拉是我的本命，我每天都要吃。
只不過我的沙拉很有個性：它堅持要加培根、要加起司絲、要淋滿滿的凱薩醬，最好旁邊再附一份薯條壯膽。

我尊重它的選擇，畢竟一份有靈魂的沙拉，本來就不該太委屈自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都點沙拉但配料比主菜還多的朋友——他會回你「這叫均衡飲食」
variations: 進階版：今天我的沙拉甚至要求加一份炸雞，我想了想，覺得不能拂逆它的心意，就加了。

en.content:
Salad is my spirit animal. I eat it every single day.
It's just that my salad has a strong personality: it insists on bacon, on shredded cheese, on a generous flood of Caesar dressing, ideally with a side of fries for moral support.

I respect its choices. A salad with a soul should never have to settle.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always orders salad but with more toppings than the main course — they'll reply 'this is called a balanced diet'
variations: Advanced version: today my salad even requested a side of fried chicken. I thought about it and decided it wasn't right to deny its wishes, so I added it.

items.sourceNote: 「After leg day I walk like I got off a horse that kicked me first」原典重寫，加台灣辦公室與機車場景在地化

zh-tw.content:
練完腿日的隔天，我走進辦公室。
同事問我：「你腳怎麼了？」

我說沒事，我只是走路像剛從一匹馬上下來——而且那匹馬下來之前還先踹了我一腳。
坐下去要分三段，站起來要喊出聲，騎機車牽車那一下，我直接看見人生跑馬燈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給昨天剛練完深蹲的朋友——他會回你「我現在連坐馬桶都要扶牆」
variations: 後續：第三天我已經練成一種全新的走路方式，同事說很像在走星光大道，只是每一步都很痛。

en.content:
The day after leg day, I walked into the office.
A coworker asked, 'what happened to your legs?'

I said nothing's wrong, I just walk like I got off a horse — a horse that kicked me first before I got down.
Sitting down takes three stages. Standing up requires an audible groan. Lifting my leg over the motorbike, I saw my whole life flash before my eyes.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who squatted yesterday — they'll reply 'I have to hold the wall just to sit on the toilet'
variations: Follow-up: by day three I'd developed an entirely new way of walking. Coworkers said it looked like a red-carpet strut, except every step hurt.

items.sourceNote: 「I stepped on the scale today, it asked for my username and password」原典重寫，加台灣體重計擬人化情境

zh-tw.content:
今天我踩上體重計。
它沉默了一下，然後跳出一行字：「請輸入帳號密碼。」

我問它為什麼，它說：「這個數字太重要，需要二次驗證。」

我默默走下來，決定這件事我們改天再談。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每天早上量完體重就懷疑人生的朋友——他會回你「我的體重計也升級了」
variations: 後續：第二天我輸入了密碼，它顯示「驗證成功，但建議您先做好心理準備」。

en.content:
I stepped on the scale this morning.
It paused, then displayed a line of text: 'Please enter your username and password.'

I asked why. It said, 'This number is too important. It requires two-factor authentication.'

I quietly stepped off and decided we'd discuss this another day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who questions their entire life after the morning weigh-in — they'll reply 'my scale got the same update'
variations: Follow-up: the next day I entered the password. It read, 'Authentication successful, but we recommend you brace yourself first.'

items.sourceNote: 「My favorite machine at the gym is the vending machine」經典格式重寫，加台灣健身房販賣機在地化

zh-tw.content:
健身房裡那麼多器材，我最熟練的是販賣機。
投幣、選擇、取貨，一氣呵成，動作標準到教練都想拍片。

別人練胸練背練腿，我練的是「手臂從口袋掏錢」那條肌肉——而且它真的越來越發達。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次去健身房都先逛販賣機的朋友——他會回你「補充能量才有力氣運動」
variations: 進階版：今天我挑戰高強度間歇——在販賣機跟按摩椅之間來回三趟，心率有上去喔。

en.content:
Of all the equipment at the gym, the machine I've truly mastered is the vending machine.
Insert coin, make a selection, collect the goods — one fluid motion, so clean my trainer wants to film it.

Everyone else trains chest, back, and legs. I train the 'pull cash out of pocket' muscle — and honestly, it just keeps getting stronger.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who visits the vending machine before touching a single weight — they'll say 'gotta refuel before working out'
variations: Pro version: today I attempted high-intensity intervals — three round trips between the vending machine and the massage chair. My heart rate definitely went up.

items.sourceNote: 「I work out because punching people is frowned upon」原典重寫，加台灣上班族通勤怒火在地化

zh-tw.content:
有人問我為什麼開始運動。
我說因為直接揍人在社會上是不被允許的。

所以我把今天塞車、被插隊、被主管已讀不回的那股氣，全部灌進沙包裡。
揍完之後我整個人神清氣爽——沙包大概不太好過，但牠是專業的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天下班都一肚子火的上班族朋友——他會回你「明天揪一起去揍沙包」
variations: 進階版：教練看我打得特別兇，問我發生什麼事，我說沒事，只是想起了今天的會議。

en.content:
Someone asked why I started working out.
I said it's because punching people in public is frowned upon.

So I take all the rage from today's traffic, the line-cutters, and the boss who left me on read, and I funnel it straight into the punching bag.
Afterward I feel completely refreshed — the bag probably had a rough time, but it's a professional.
en.editorNote: Send to the office friend who clocks out furious every day — they'll reply 'let's go hit the bag together tomorrow'
variations: Pro version: my trainer noticed how hard I was hitting and asked what happened. I said nothing — I just remembered today's meeting.

items.sourceNote: 「The added fear of being murdered really does wonders for my cardio / I don't do sprints, I panic slowly」格式重寫，加台灣夜跑與便利商店在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的有氧運動很特別：我不衝刺，我「緩慢地驚慌」。
夜跑經過沒路燈的那段，身後傳來一點點聲音，我的配速立刻進步了三十秒。

原來我缺的從來不是意志力，是一點點恐懼——
下次乾脆請朋友躲在巷口假裝要追我，效果保證比教練還好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次夜跑都被自己嚇到加速的朋友——他會回你「便利商店那段我跑超快」
variations: 後續：今天那個聲音其實只是塑膠袋，但我已經破個人紀錄了，所以我決定假裝牠是真的。

en.content:
My cardio routine is unique: I don't sprint, I panic slowly.
On the unlit stretch of my night run, a faint noise behind me improved my pace by thirty seconds instantly.

Turns out what I was missing all along wasn't willpower — it was a little fear.
Next time I'll just ask a friend to hide at the corner and pretend to chase me. Guaranteed better results than any trainer.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who scares themselves into a faster pace every night run — they'll reply 'I sprint past the convenience store every time'
variations: Follow-up: today the noise turned out to be a plastic bag, but I'd already set a personal record, so I've decided to pretend it was real.

items.sourceNote: 「The scale doesn't lie, but we agreed never to speak again」原典重寫，加台灣體重計冷戰擬人化情境

zh-tw.content:
體重計從來不說謊，這是牠最大的優點，也是我們鬧翻的原因。

上禮拜牠對我說了實話，從那天起我們就沒再講過話。
牠安靜地躺在浴室角落，我安靜地繞道走。

我們達成了一個成熟的共識：保持距離，互不打擾，這段關係才能長久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給跟體重計處於冷戰狀態的朋友——他會回你「我已經把它收進櫃子裡了」
variations: 進階版：今天我終於主動踩上去想和好，結果牠顯示的數字讓我們又冷戰了一個禮拜。

en.content:
The scale never lies. That's its greatest virtue, and also the reason we fell out.

Last week it told me the truth, and we haven't spoken since.
It lies quietly in the corner of the bathroom. I quietly take the long way around.

We've reached a mature understanding: keep our distance, leave each other alone — that's the only way this relationship survives.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a cold war with their bathroom scale — they'll reply 'I already put mine away in the cabinet'
variations: Pro version: today I finally stepped on it to make peace. The number it showed sent us right back into another week of silence.

items.sourceNote: 「my weight fluctuates more than my Wi-Fi signal」格式重寫，加台灣家用網路在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的體重起伏，比我家的 Wi-Fi 訊號還不穩。
早上量一個數字，晚上量又是另一個，跟我家網速一樣，全看心情。

唯一的差別是：網路斷線我會想辦法修，體重亂跳我只會關掉螢幕假裝沒看到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天量三次體重的朋友——他會回你「我家網路也是這樣」
variations: 進階版：聽說兩個都會因為『連線品質變好』而改善——可惜我跟食物的連線一直都很穩。

en.content:
My weight fluctuates more than my Wi-Fi signal.
One number in the morning, a completely different one at night — just like my internet speed, entirely mood-dependent.

The only difference: when the Wi-Fi drops, I try to fix it. When my weight jumps, I just turn off the screen and pretend I saw nothing.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who weighs themselves three times a day — they'll reply 'my router does this too'
variations: Pro version: apparently both improve with a 'better connection'. Unfortunately my connection to food has never once dropped.

items.sourceNote: 「never shop hangry, it's a recipe for heavy groceries」原典重寫，加台灣超市與便利商店在地化

zh-tw.content:
減肥最重要的一條規則：肚子餓的時候不要去超市。

我餓著肚子進去買一瓶醬油，出來的時候推車裡多了餅乾、冰淇淋、兩包洋芋片，跟一盒原本不存在於我人生計畫裡的雞蛋糕。

那瓶醬油，後來我忘了買。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次空腹逛超市就大失血的朋友——他會立刻看一眼自己昨天的購物明細
variations: 進階版：後來我學乖了，餓的時候改去便利商店——結果連關東煮的湯都喝了兩碗。

en.content:
The most important diet rule: never go grocery shopping hungry.

I went in starving to buy one bottle of soy sauce. I came out with cookies, ice cream, two bags of chips, and a box of egg cakes that had never once existed in my life plan.

The soy sauce? I forgot to buy it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who hemorrhages money shopping on an empty stomach — they'll glance at yesterday's receipt
variations: Pro version: I learned my lesson and switched to the convenience store when hungry. I ended up drinking two bowls of the hot pot broth at the counter.

items.sourceNote: 「rest days are my strongest muscle」格式重寫，加台灣沙發追劇在地化

zh-tw.content:
別人問我練得最好的部位是哪裡。
我認真想了想，回答：休息日。

這個肌群我每天都有在練，從不偷懶，強度逐年提升——
現在已經可以一口氣在沙發上連休七天，追完一整部劇都不抽筋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給把『休息也是訓練的一部分』掛在嘴邊的朋友——他會回你「我這部位早就練滿了」
variations: 進階版：教練說要練到力竭，我說我躺在沙發上就已經很接近力竭的感覺了。

en.content:
Someone asked which muscle group I've developed the most.
I thought about it seriously and answered: rest days.

I train this group every single day, never skip it, and the intensity increases year over year —
I can now rest on the couch for seven days straight and binge an entire series without a single cramp.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'rest is part of training too' — they'll reply 'I maxed out that muscle ages ago'
variations: Pro version: my trainer said to push to failure. I told him lying on the couch already feels pretty close to failure.

items.sourceNote: 「my protein shake tastes like regret」格式重寫，加台灣高蛋白與手搖飲對比在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的乳清蛋白喝起來有一種味道，後來我查了一下，那個味道叫做「後悔」。

為了健康，我每天忍著喝完一杯，臉皺得像在做臉部運動。
結果隔壁同事的奶茶半糖少冰，香得讓我懷疑自己的人生選擇。

所以我決定折衷：蛋白粉照喝，奶茶照點，兩個都不辜負。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天逼自己喝高蛋白的健身朋友——他會回你「我今天偷喝奶茶了」
variations: 進階版：後來我把蛋白粉加進奶茶裡，告訴自己這叫『機能飲料』——口感意外地不差，罪惡感意外地翻倍。

en.content:
My protein shake has a certain flavor. I looked it up — the flavor is called 'regret'.

For my health, I force one down every day, face scrunched like I'm doing facial exercises.
Meanwhile the coworker next to me has a half-sugar milk tea so fragrant it makes me question every life choice I've ever made.

So I reached a compromise: keep the protein, keep the milk tea, disappoint neither.
en.editorNote: Send to the gym friend who chokes down protein daily — they'll reply 'I snuck a milk tea today'
variations: Pro version: I started mixing the protein powder into my milk tea and called it a 'functional beverage'. Surprisingly drinkable. The guilt, surprisingly, doubled.

items.sourceNote: 「my treadmill is just an expensive clothes hanger」原典重寫，加台灣居家健身器材在地化

zh-tw.content:
三年前我買了一台跑步機，想說在家就能運動，多方便。

結果這三年來，它最常承擔的重量，是我掛在扶手上的外套、毛巾、跟昨天還沒收的那件襯衫。

它現在是全家最貴的一支曬衣架，而且還會發出『嗶』一聲提醒我：曾經有人對它抱有期待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家裡也有一台變成曬衣架的健身器材的朋友——他會傳一張自家現況給你
variations: 進階版：後來我把它賣掉，買的人說要拿來認真跑步——我看著他充滿希望的眼神，默默幫他祈禱。

en.content:
Three years ago I bought a treadmill, thinking I could work out at home. So convenient.

Over those three years, the weight it has most often carried is the jacket draped over the handrail, plus a towel, plus the shirt I still haven't put away from yesterday.

It's now the most expensive clothes rack in the house, and it still beeps once to remind me that someone, once, had hopes for it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose home gym equipment also turned into a drying rack — they'll send a photo of its current state
variations: Pro version: I eventually sold it. The buyer said they were going to run on it seriously. I looked into their hopeful eyes and quietly said a prayer for them.

items.sourceNote: 「running late counts as cardio」遲到等於有氧的自嘲格式重寫，加台灣通勤在地化

zh-tw.content:
我今天有做有氧喔。
早上睡過頭，從捷運站一路衝去公司，紅燈也照樣穿越，包包甩到後面當配重。

所以說，認真運動的人不一定要去健身房——
你只要每天遲到，這座城市就是你的跑步機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天上班都用百米衝刺進公司的同事——他會回你「我今天破個人紀錄」
variations: 進階版：心率手錶今天顯示我做了 28 分鐘高強度間歇——其實那是在追一班我永遠趕不上的公車。

en.content:
I did cardio today.
I overslept, sprinted from the station to the office, crossed on red lights, and let my bag swing behind me as ballast.

So really, serious athletes don't always need a gym —
just be late every day, and the whole city becomes your treadmill.
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who arrives at work in a full sprint — they'll reply 'set a personal best today'
variations: Pro version: my watch logged 28 minutes of high-intensity intervals today. That was me chasing a bus I was never going to catch.

items.sourceNote: 「skipping dessert is my cardio」跳過甜點當有氧的格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲在地化

zh-tw.content:
今天我成功跳過了一杯手搖飲，這就是我今天的有氧。
決定不點的那一刻，心跳加速、手心冒汗、內心天人交戰——
各項生理指標完全符合運動定義。

所以我等於做完運動了，獎勵自己一杯珍奶剛剛好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次經過手搖店都要掙扎三秒的朋友——他會回你「我今天輸在最後一步」
variations: 進階版：我連續三天跳過甜點，根據能量守恆，我已經累積足夠額度可以一次吃一整個蛋糕。

en.content:
I successfully skipped a bubble tea today. That was my cardio.
The moment I decided not to order, my heart raced, my palms sweated, an inner war broke out —
every vital sign perfectly matched the definition of exercise.

So I've basically finished my workout. A bubble tea reward feels just right.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who battles themselves for three seconds outside every drink shop — they'll reply 'I lost at the final step today'
variations: Pro version: I've skipped dessert three days running. By energy conservation, I've now banked enough credit to eat an entire cake in one sitting.

items.sourceNote: 「I named my diet Thor, it always thunders when I cheat」作弊日打雷格式重寫，加台灣夜市在地化

zh-tw.content:
我幫我的減肥計畫取了個名字，叫做「雷神」。
因為每次我破戒，天空就會打雷。

上禮拜我在夜市一口氣吃了蚵仔煎、大腸包小腸、再加一份地瓜球，
那天晚上雷聲大到鄰居都出來看——
我知道，那不是天氣，那是我的計畫在哭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次說好只逛夜市不吃結果掃街的朋友——他會回你「昨天雷超大」
variations: 後續：我把計畫改名叫「氣象局」，因為它現在連我想破戒都能提前預報。

en.content:
I named my diet plan 'Thor'.
Because every time I cheat, thunder rolls across the sky.

Last week I demolished an oyster omelet, a sausage wrap, and a basket of sweet potato balls at the night market in one go.
The thunder that night was so loud the neighbors came out to look —
I knew it wasn't the weather. It was my plan, weeping.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who swears they'll only window-shop the night market and then eats the whole street — they'll reply 'big thunder last night'
variations: Follow-up: I renamed the plan 'the weather bureau', because it now forecasts my cheat days before I've even decided.

items.sourceNote: 「I think I found an ab muscle, oh wait that's last night's taco」找到腹肌結果是宵夜格式重寫，加台灣宵夜在地化

zh-tw.content:
今天洗澡的時候，我摸到肚子上有一塊硬硬的——
我超興奮，以為是練了三個月終於長出來的腹肌。

冷靜一下才想起來，那是昨晚的鹹酥雞加滷味，還沒消化完。

原來我離腹肌最近的一次，是消化不良。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次運動完都偷偷摸肚子找腹肌的朋友——他會回你「我也摸到一塊，是水餃」
variations: 進階版：後來我去問教練，他說那叫『偽腹肌』，學名是『昨晚的選擇』。

en.content:
In the shower today I felt something firm on my stomach —
I got so excited, sure it was the ab I'd been training three months for.

Then I remembered: that's last night's fried chicken and braised snacks, still undigested.

Turns out the closest I've ever come to abs is indigestion.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who secretly pokes their stomach for abs after every workout — they'll reply 'felt one too, it was dumplings'
variations: Pro version: I asked my trainer about it. He said it's called a 'pseudo-ab', scientific name 'last night's decisions'.

items.sourceNote: 水重 / 喝水也胖的自欺格式原創語錄，加台灣手搖飲在地化

zh-tw.content:
我最近重了兩公斤，但我查過了，這完全是「水重」。

畢竟我昨天喝了水、喝了湯、喝了珍奶、喝了啤酒、半夜還喝了一杯豆漿。
人體有百分之七十是水，而我，可能高達百分之九十。

所以這不是胖，這是我比一般人更有水準。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次體重上升都怪水重的朋友——他會立刻回你「對啦一定是水」
variations: 進階版：醫生請我把『水』的定義縮小一點，我說好，但珍珠也是固體的水，這點我堅持。

en.content:
I gained two kilos recently, but I looked it up — it's all 'water weight'.

After all, yesterday I drank water, soup, bubble tea, beer, and a glass of soy milk at midnight.
The human body is 70 percent water. I, personally, might be running closer to 90.

So this isn't fat. I'm simply more hydrated than the average person.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who blames every weight gain on water — they'll reply instantly 'right, it's definitely the water'
variations: Pro version: the doctor asked me to narrow my definition of 'water'. I agreed, but tapioca pearls are technically solid water, and on that I stand firm.

items.sourceNote: 「節食第二天比第一天輕鬆」經典格式重寫，加台灣語感

zh-tw.content:
大家都說減肥第二天會比第一天輕鬆，這句話完全正確。

因為到了第二天，我已經放棄了，整個人非常輕鬆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給昨天才信誓旦旦要減肥、今天已經在訂炸雞的朋友
variations: 第三天更輕鬆了，因為我連『我在減肥』這件事都忘記了。

en.content:
Everyone says the second day of a diet is easier than the first. They're absolutely right.

By the second day I've already quit, so honestly I feel super relaxed.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who swore they'd diet yesterday and is already ordering fried chicken today
variations: Day three is even easier, because by then I've forgotten I was ever on a diet.

items.sourceNote: 「健身就是把披薩塞進嘴裡」雙關格式原創重寫

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我有沒有在健身。

我說有啊，我很努力在「健身」——具體來說，是努力把整塊雞排健身進我嘴裡。

動作標準，組數很滿，從不偷懶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 健身房打卡照配文，給那種發限動都在吃的朋友最有共感
variations: 今天加練了：把第二塊雞排也健身進去，這叫漸進式超負荷。

en.content:
A friend asked if I've been working out.

Of course I have. I work out really hard — specifically, I work this entire fried chicken cutlet into my mouth.

Perfect form, full sets, never skip a rep.
en.editorNote: Caption for a gym check-in photo, perfect for the friend whose stories are all food
variations: Added an extra set today: worked a second cutlet in too. They call that progressive overload.

items.sourceNote: 「站在沙拉前希望它是甜甜圈」格式在地化重寫

zh-tw.content:
我只是一個平凡的人，站在一盤沙拉面前，
誠心誠意地祈求它變成一份滷肉飯。

沙拉看著我，沒有回應。我們都知道，奇蹟不會發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 午餐被迫吃沙拉時發限動配文，配一張生菜照最哀傷
variations: 後來沙拉真的沒變，倒是我手滑點了旁邊的滷肉飯，所以這也算心想事成。

en.content:
I'm just an ordinary person, standing in front of a bowl of salad,
sincerely praying for it to turn into a plate of braised pork rice.

The salad stares back. No response. We both know miracles don't happen here.
en.editorNote: Caption for the story you post when forced to eat salad for lunch — pair it with a sad photo of lettuce
variations: The salad never changed, but my hand slipped and ordered the pork rice next to it, so technically the wish came true.

items.sourceNote: 「體重計要求帳號密碼」吐槽格式原創重寫

zh-tw.content:
今天早上踩上體重計，它沉默了三秒，
然後跳出一行字：「請輸入帳號密碼，本系統需要驗證您的身分。」

我懂了，它是在委婉地告訴我，它不敢直接顯示這個數字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 量完體重崩潰時自嘲用，傳給每次站上體重計都要先深呼吸的朋友
variations: 我輸入密碼後，它又跳出：「系統繁忙，請稍後再試。」連體重計都想逃避現實。

en.content:
I stepped on the scale this morning. It went quiet for three seconds,
then flashed a line of text: 'Please enter your username and password. This system requires identity verification.'

I get it. It's politely telling me it doesn't dare display this number directly.
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating caption for post-weigh-in despair — send to the friend who takes a deep breath before every weigh-in
variations: After I entered my password it said 'System busy, please try again later.' Even the scale wants to avoid reality.

items.sourceNote: 「lunge加crunch等於lunch」雙關，中文以在地化諧音重寫

zh-tw.content:
我最喜歡的運動，是弓箭步加捲腹的綜合動作。

做著做著肚子餓了，於是我把這套動作改名叫「便當」。

現在我每天認真做三餐份的便當，從不間斷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一說要運動就先去買飯的朋友，健身餐照片配文也很合
variations: 進階課程：弓箭步、捲腹、再加一個開冰箱，這套我稱之為『宵夜全餐』。

en.content:
My favorite workout is a combo of lunges and crunches.

Halfway through I get hungry, so I renamed the whole routine 'lunch'.

Now I do three full meals' worth every day, without fail.
en.editorNote: For the friend who buys food the second they mention exercising — also works as a meal-prep photo caption
variations: Advanced course: lunge, crunch, then open the fridge. I call that one the 'midnight snack circuit'.

items.sourceNote: 經典「seafood diet：see food and eat it」雙關，中文以在地化重寫並補上情境

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我最近在執行什麼飲食法，
我很認真地說：「看到法。」

看到什麼就吃什麼，從不挑食，這份堅持已經維持了二十八年，從來沒破功過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問減肥進度時的萬用回覆，傳給那種嘴上喊減肥、手上拿鹹酥雞的朋友
variations: 升級版叫『聞到法』，連看到都不用，聞到香味就自動進廚房。

en.content:
A friend asked what diet I've been following lately.

I told them, dead serious: 'The see-food diet.'

I see food, I eat it. No exceptions, no cheat days. I've kept this up for twenty-eight years without a single slip.
en.editorNote: An all-purpose reply when someone asks about your weight-loss progress — send to the friend who preaches dieting with fried chicken in hand
variations: The upgraded version is the smell-food diet. You don't even have to see it — one whiff and your legs walk you to the kitchen.

items.sourceNote: 「不是百分百完美就乾脆放棄」減肥心態梗，重寫成自嘲式內心戲

zh-tw.content:
我的減肥計畫只有兩種狀態：百分之百完美，或是徹底躺平。

今天早餐多吃了一口蛋餅，整個系統判定本日已失敗，
於是順理成章地點了炸雞當午餐、珍奶當晚餐，明天再重新開始。

明天這個檔案，我已經存了不知道幾百份了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 破戒後安慰自己用，傳給每次吃一口就覺得整天毀了的完美主義朋友
variations: 我的人生只有『今天開始』和『明天再說』兩個按鈕，而且我的手指好像只找得到第二個。

en.content:
My diet plan has exactly two settings: one hundred percent flawless, or completely lying on the floor.

This morning I took one extra bite of pancake, so the whole system flagged today as a failure.

Naturally, that meant fried chicken for lunch, bubble tea for dinner, and a fresh start tomorrow.

I've saved that 'tomorrow' file a few hundred times by now.
en.editorNote: For comforting yourself after slipping up — send to the perfectionist friend who thinks one bite ruins the entire day
variations: My life only has two buttons: 'start today' and 'deal with it tomorrow' — and my finger somehow only finds the second one.

items.sourceNote: 健身房厭世動機梗，重寫成自嘲式健身房內心獨白

zh-tw.content:
教練說：「想像三個月後那個更好的你。」

我閉上眼睛認真想像了一下，
結果那個更好的我，正坐在沙發上吃洋芋片，對著現在流汗的我比讚。

他看起來很快樂，我決定向他學習。
zh-tw.editorNote: 健身房打卡照配文，傳給每次運動五分鐘就開始懷疑人生的健身夥伴
variations: 我問未來的我有沒有瘦下來，他嘴巴塞滿東西，含糊地說『下一個三個月一定』。

en.content:
My trainer said, 'Picture the better version of you three months from now.'

So I closed my eyes and really pictured it.

Turns out the better me was sitting on the couch eating chips, giving a thumbs-up to the sweaty me at the gym.

He looked really happy. I've decided to learn from him.
en.editorNote: Caption for a gym check-in photo — send to the workout buddy who questions everything five minutes into a session
variations: I asked future me if I'd lost the weight. Mouth full of food, he mumbled, 'definitely next three months.'

items.sourceNote: 「沙拉只是配菜/dressing」雙關梗，中文在地化為手搖與正餐的對比

zh-tw.content:
我其實有在吃沙拉，每餐都有。

只是我的沙拉比較特別，主角是炸排骨，配角是滷蛋，
那把生菜負責站在便當角落，象徵我對健康的一份心意。

它從來沒被吃掉，但它的存在讓我心安。
zh-tw.editorNote: 點餐被酸不健康時自嘲用，傳給每個便當都會留一角生菜當裝飾的朋友
variations: 那把生菜後來變成我的固定室友，每天陪我吃飯，但我們從不交流。

en.content:
I do eat salad, actually. Every single meal.

Mine's just a little different — the lead role goes to fried pork chop, supporting cast is a braised egg,
and that handful of lettuce stands in the corner of the lunchbox, representing my heartfelt commitment to health.

It never gets eaten, but its presence brings me peace.
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating reply when someone calls out your unhealthy order — send to the friend whose every lunchbox has a decorative corner of greens
variations: That handful of lettuce eventually became my permanent roommate. It joins me for every meal, but we never speak.

items.sourceNote: 「體重計不會說謊，但我們再也不講話了」梗，重寫成冷戰式關係敘事

zh-tw.content:
體重計從來不說謊，這點我承認。

所以我跟它約好了：以後井水不犯河水，
它不主動告訴我數字，我也不踩上去逼問它。

我們現在維持著一種成熟的冷戰，浴室因此和平了很多。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種每天早上量體重然後生一整天悶氣的朋友，叫他放過自己也放過體重計
variations: 我把體重計收進櫃子最深處，眼不見為淨，這叫做斷捨離。
它最後一次說的數字太傷人，我們從此各過各的，互不打擾。

en.content:
The scale never lies. I'll give it that.

So we made a deal: we stay out of each other's business.
It doesn't volunteer the number, and I don't step on it to interrogate it.

We're maintaining a very mature cold war now, and the bathroom is a much more peaceful place for it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who weighs in every morning and then sulks all day — tell them to let themselves, and the scale, off the hook
variations: I moved the scale to the very back of the cabinet. Out of sight, out of mind. I'm calling it decluttering.
The last number it gave me was too hurtful. We go our separate ways now and don't bother each other.

items.sourceNote: 「健身服比減肥計畫還貴」與運動後甜點梗結合，重寫成裝備派內心話

zh-tw.content:
我買了一整套機能運動服，吸濕排汗、修飾線條、看起來超專業。

它們唯一的功能，就是讓我躺在沙發上滑手機的時候，
看起來像一個隨時都會去運動的人。

至今還沒有一件流過汗，它們和我一樣，都在等一個對的時機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 買齊裝備卻遲遲沒開始運動時自嘲用，傳給衣櫥裡運動服比運動次數多的朋友
variations: 我的運動服已經三個月沒沾過汗了，但它們把我襯托得非常有運動員氣質。
別人靠運動瘦，我靠買運動服的瞬間，就先在心裡瘦了一公斤。

en.content:
I bought a full set of performance workout gear — moisture-wicking, body-flattering, looks incredibly professional.

Its only actual function is to make me look like a person who's about to go work out
while I lie on the couch scrolling my phone.

Not a single piece has seen sweat yet. They're just like me — waiting for the right moment.
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating caption for when you've bought all the gear but haven't started — send to the friend whose closet has more activewear than actual workouts
variations: My workout clothes haven't touched sweat in three months, but they give me a very convincing athlete vibe.
Other people lose weight by exercising. I lose a pound mentally the moment I buy new gym clothes.

items.sourceNote: 「海鮮減肥法／see food」雙關，中文在地化為諧音與看到就吃的衝動

zh-tw.content:
有人問我在執行什麼減肥法，我說我走的是「視覺系」。

原理很簡單：眼睛看到食物，大腦立刻下達指令，嘴巴負責執行。

從決定到吃完，全程不超過三秒，效率高到連我自己都佩服。

唯一的副作用，是這套方法從來沒讓我瘦過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 被問「你到底在減什麼肥」時的標準回答，傳給看到美食照就餓的朋友
variations: 我的減肥計畫很視覺導向：看得到的都算我的份。
別人計算卡路里，我計算從看到到吃完的反應時間，目前最佳紀錄兩秒。

en.content:
Someone asked what diet I'm on. I told them I follow the 'see-food' method.

The principle is simple: my eyes spot food, my brain issues the order, my mouth carries it out.

From decision to clean plate takes under three seconds — an efficiency that honestly impresses even me.

The only side effect is that it has never, not once, made me thinner.
en.editorNote: Standard reply for when someone asks what diet you're actually on — send to the friend who gets hungry just looking at food photos
variations: My diet plan is very visual: if I can see it, it counts as my portion.
Other people count calories. I count my reaction time from spotting food to finishing it. Current record: two seconds.

items.sourceNote: 「我把蛋糕全吃了，因為我有始有終」梗，重寫成自我合理化的決心宣言

zh-tw.content:
我把整塊蛋糕吃完了，一口都沒剩。

不是因為我餓，是因為我這個人最大的優點，就是有始有終。

做事不能半途而廢，這是做人的基本原則，
蛋糕只是剛好成為我貫徹這份決心的犧牲品。

你看，連減肥失敗，我都失敗得很有原則。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把整盒甜點解決掉後的自我安慰用語，傳給那種說好只吃一口結果吃光的朋友
variations: 我從不浪費食物，這是美德，跟我的腰圍無關。
別人靠意志力拒絕甜點，我靠意志力把它吃完，殊途同歸，都是堅持。

en.content:
I finished the entire cake. Didn't leave a single bite.

Not because I was hungry, but because my single greatest virtue is following through.

You should never leave a thing half-done — it's a basic life principle,
and the cake just happened to be the casualty of my commitment.

See? Even when I fail at dieting, I fail with principles.
en.editorNote: Self-soothing line for after you've polished off the whole dessert — send to the friend who 'just has one bite' and finishes the box
variations: I never waste food. That's a virtue, and it has nothing to do with my waistline.
Other people use willpower to refuse dessert. I use willpower to finish it. Same path, different ending — both are perseverance.

items.sourceNote: 「我運動是因為我真的很愛吃／lunch=lunge+crunch」梗，重寫成運動與進食的閉環邏輯

zh-tw.content:
別人問我為什麼這麼勤勞上健身房，
我都很誠實地回答：因為我真的、真的很愛吃。

我練的不是腹肌，是吃下一餐的資格。
運動只是前菜，那頓飯才是我今天的主線任務。

所以嚴格來說，我不是在健身，我是在儲值。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那種運動完馬上問「等下吃什麼」的健身夥伴，他會點頭如搗蒜
variations: 我把跑步機當成提款機，跑得越久，晚餐能領的額度就越高。
教練問我目標是什麼，我說：練到可以心安理得地吃宵夜。

en.content:
When people ask why I'm so diligent about hitting the gym,
I always answer honestly: because I really, really love food.

I'm not training abs. I'm training my right to eat the next meal.
The workout is just the appetizer — dinner is today's actual main quest.

So technically, I'm not exercising. I'm topping up my balance.
en.editorNote: Send to the workout buddy who asks 'so what are we eating after this' mid-set — they'll nod furiously
variations: I treat the treadmill like an ATM. The longer I run, the bigger the dinner I'm allowed to withdraw.
My trainer asked what my goal is. I said: to earn the right to eat a midnight snack guilt-free.

items.sourceNote: 「蔬菜很重要，我推薦紅蘿蔔蛋糕／南瓜派」梗，重寫成偷換概念的飲食清單

zh-tw.content:
減肥當然要多吃蔬菜，這點我完全同意。

所以我精心安排了一份均衡菜單：
紅蘿蔔蛋糕、櫛瓜麵包、南瓜派，必要時再來碗玉米濃湯。

你看，每一樣的原料表上都印著蔬菜，
營養師看了會落淚，但落的是哪種淚我就不確定了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 幫自己的甜點清單找一個植物性的正當理由時用，傳給總說要多吃菜的朋友
variations: 我的五蔬果指標靠的是蔬菜口味的洋芋片，技術上也算有過。
番茄是蔬菜，所以我點披薩其實是在攝取蔬菜，邏輯非常通順。

en.content:
Eating more vegetables is essential when you're dieting — I fully agree.

So I've carefully put together a balanced menu:
carrot cake, zucchini bread, pumpkin pie, and a bowl of corn chowder when needed.

Look closely — every single ingredient list mentions a vegetable.
A nutritionist would weep, though I'm not entirely sure which kind of tears.
en.editorNote: For when you need a plant-based justification for your dessert lineup — send to the friend who always says to eat more greens
variations: I hit my five-a-day with vegetable-flavored chips. Technically that still counts.
Tomatoes are a vegetable, so ordering pizza is really just a way to eat my veggies. The logic is airtight.

items.sourceNote: 「運動？我以為你說的是薯條」聽錯梗，重寫成大腦自動過濾健康詞彙

zh-tw.content:
朋友認真對我說：「你該開始運動了。」
我眼睛一亮，回他：「好啊，加大薯條對吧？」

他愣了三秒，說他講的是運動。
我也愣了三秒，因為我大腦顯然自動把那兩個字翻譯成了薯條。

醫生說這叫選擇性聽力，我覺得這叫忠於自我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聽到「運動」自動聯想成美食時的自白，傳給聽到健康關鍵字就自動消音的朋友
variations: 我的耳朵有個內建濾鏡，所有跟流汗有關的詞都會被翻譯成餐點。
別人聽到「要不要去跑步」會起身，我聽到的是「要不要去吃滷味」。

en.content:
A friend told me, dead serious: 'You should start exercising.'
My eyes lit up and I said: 'Great — extra fries, right?'

They froze for three seconds and clarified they meant exercise.
I froze for three seconds too, because my brain had clearly auto-translated that word into fries.

The doctor calls it selective hearing. I call it staying true to myself.
en.editorNote: A confession for when 'exercise' automatically registers as food — send to the friend whose brain mutes every health-related keyword
variations: My ears have a built-in filter that translates every sweat-related word into a meal.
Other people hear 'wanna go for a run?' and stand up. I hear 'wanna go grab snacks?'

items.sourceNote: 「遲到算不算有氧／running late as cardio」梗，重寫成把日常狼狽包裝成運動量

zh-tw.content:
有人問我今天有沒有運動，我說當然有，而且強度很高。

早上趕公車衝刺一百公尺，這是無氧爆發力訓練；
追垃圾車那段，標準的間歇有氧；
提兩袋飲料上五樓，負重訓練無誤。

我從不去健身房，因為我的人生本身就是一場障礙賽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把每天的兵荒馬亂換算成運動量時的精神勝利法，傳給總說沒時間運動的朋友
variations: 我今天的步數來自找停車位繞了八圈，這也算燃脂吧。
趕捷運轉乘那段跑步機根本比不上，我每天都在做免費的高強度間歇。

en.content:
Someone asked if I worked out today. Of course I did, and at high intensity too.

Sprinting a hundred meters for the morning bus? That's explosive anaerobic training.
Chasing down the garbage truck? Textbook interval cardio.
Carrying two bags of drinks up to the fifth floor? Weighted resistance, no question.

I never go to the gym, because my life is already an obstacle course.
en.editorNote: A mental-victory method for converting your daily chaos into a workout — send to the friend who always says they have no time to exercise
variations: Today's step count came from circling the lot eight times for parking. That's fat-burning too, right?
No treadmill beats sprinting for a metro transfer. I do free high-intensity intervals every single day.

items.sourceNote: 「我有個無法節食的病，叫做肚子餓」梗，重寫成把食慾包裝成不可抗力的醫療理由

zh-tw.content:
我很想節食，真的，但我有個無法克服的病。

這個病的症狀是：每隔幾小時就會發作一次，
發作時全身無力、注意力渙散、脾氣暴躁，
唯一的解藥是食物，而且份量越大療效越好。

醫學上這叫肚子餓，但我覺得這個說法太輕描淡寫了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 幫自己破功的食慾找一個聽起來很嚴重的病名時用，傳給一餓就六親不認的朋友
variations: 我不是貪吃，我只是有一種需要規律進食才能維持人格穩定的慢性狀況。
醫生建議我少量多餐，我聽成了多餐就好，效果非常顯著。

en.content:
I'd love to diet, I really would, but I have a condition I just can't beat.

The symptoms: it flares up every few hours,
leaving me weak, unable to focus, and short-tempered,
and the only cure is food — the larger the dose, the better it works.

Medically they call it being hungry, but honestly that label undersells it.
en.editorNote: For when you need a serious-sounding diagnosis for your blown willpower — send to the friend who forgets they have manners the moment they're hungry
variations: I'm not greedy. I just have a chronic condition that requires regular feeding to keep my personality stable.
The doctor advised small, frequent meals. I heard 'frequent meals' and the results have been remarkable.

items.sourceNote: 「睡覺是最好的減肥運動，因為睡著就吃不了東西」哏重寫

zh-tw.content:
我終於找到一項零失敗的減肥運動了——睡覺。

躺平的那八個小時，是我一天當中唯一沒有偷吃的時段。
清醒，才是我最大的肥胖風險因子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一邊喊減肥、一邊半夜爬起來找東西吃的朋友，順便祝他早點睡
variations: 醫生問我運動量夠不夠，我說我每天規律進行八小時的高效率燃脂——睡眠。
減肥成功的秘訣其實很簡單：在你想吃東西之前先睡著。

en.content:
I've finally found a fail-proof weight loss exercise: sleeping.

Those eight hours flat on my back are the only stretch of the day when I'm not sneaking a snack.
Being awake is, statistically, my single biggest risk factor.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who swears they're dieting but still raids the kitchen at 2 a.m. — and wish them an early bedtime
variations: My doctor asked if I get enough exercise. I said I do eight solid hours of high-efficiency fat burning every night, called sleep.
The secret to losing weight is simple: just fall asleep before you get the urge to eat.

items.sourceNote: 「Stressed 倒過來就是 Desserts」雙語雙關重寫

zh-tw.content:
把「壓力」這兩個字翻過來看，剛好就是「甜點」。

我不是嘴饞，我只是在用最科學的方式紓壓。
蛋糕不是敵人，是治療師。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下午茶時間配一張甜點照發群組，替自己的第二塊提拉米蘇找一個高尚的理由
variations: 壓力大的時候吃甜點，是身體的自我保護機制，我只是很聽身體的話而已。
醫生說要減壓，我照辦了——我減的是壓力，增的是體重。

en.content:
Spell 'stressed' backwards and you get 'desserts.'

That's not a coincidence, that's a prescription.
The cheesecake isn't my weakness, it's my therapist.
en.editorNote: Pair with a dessert photo at teatime to justify going back for a second slice of tiramisu
variations: Eating dessert when I'm stressed is just my body protecting itself. I'm simply a very obedient patient.
My doctor told me to reduce stress. I did — I reduced the stress and increased the weight.

items.sourceNote: 「在車上吃掉的不算數」卡路里會計學新角度重寫

zh-tw.content:
卡路里會計準則補充條款：
在車上吃完、到家前消滅的食物，視為從未發生的交易。

畢竟沒有人看到，連發票都沒開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總愛在停好車後、進家門前默默清空一整袋鹽酥雞的朋友
variations: 站在冰箱前直接吃掉的，也不算——因為我根本沒坐下，等於沒進食。
別人請客的卡路里也不計入個人帳本，那是公司支出，不是私人開銷。

en.content:
Amendment to the calorie accounting standards:
anything eaten in the car and finished before you reach the front door counts as a transaction that never happened.

No witnesses, no receipt, no calories.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who quietly demolishes a whole bag of fried chicken after parking but before walking inside
variations: Food eaten standing in front of the fridge doesn't count either — I never sat down, so technically I never ate.
Calories from a meal someone else paid for go on the company books, not my personal ledger.

items.sourceNote: 「銀行帳戶在谷底、體重在巔峰」反差金句重寫

zh-tw.content:
我的人生有兩個數字，剛好走在相反的方向。

銀行帳戶的數字努力往谷底衝刺，
體重計的數字則勇敢攀上人生巔峰。

至少有一個是一直在成長的，這樣想就釋懷多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底看完帳單又站上體重計時，發到限動配一張無奈自拍最對味
variations: 存款在減肥，體重在增肌，我這個人整體來說非常平衡。
錢包越來越瘦，我卻越來越有份量，宇宙的能量守恆從不缺席。

en.content:
My life runs on two numbers, and they're heading in opposite directions.

My bank balance is sprinting toward rock bottom,
while the number on the scale is bravely scaling new heights.

At least one of them is consistently growing. Honestly, that's comforting.
en.editorNote: Post to your story with a defeated selfie right after you've checked your end-of-month bill and stepped on the scale
variations: My savings are on a diet, my body is bulking. As a whole, I'm extremely well balanced.
My wallet keeps getting thinner while I keep gaining substance. Conservation of energy never takes a day off.

items.sourceNote: 「逛街別餓肚子，否則買回一堆重的東西」哏重寫

zh-tw.content:
減肥的第一條鐵律，跟食物無關，跟逛街有關：

千萬不要餓著肚子去超市。
餓著進去，推車裡會莫名其妙多出三包餅乾、兩盒泡麵，和一桶你根本不認識的冰淇淋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次空腹採買、回家才發現買了一堆零食的室友，附上一句「我懂」
variations: 肚子餓的時候眼睛會自動把整間賣場標成『需要』，理智則默默離線。
結帳那一刻才驚覺：我來買晚餐食材，怎麼變成搬了一週的宵夜回家。

en.content:
The first iron rule of dieting has nothing to do with food and everything to do with shopping:

never, ever go to the grocery store hungry.
Walk in starving and the cart mysteriously fills with three bags of cookies, two packs of instant noodles, and a tub of ice cream you don't even recognize.
en.editorNote: Send to the roommate who shops on an empty stomach and only notices the snack haul once they're home — add a simple 'I get it'
variations: When you're hungry, your eyes label the entire store as 'essential' while your common sense quietly logs off.
I went in for dinner ingredients and somehow checked out with a week's worth of midnight snacks.

items.sourceNote: 經典「海鮮減肥法 see food」雙關哏，重寫成自白格式

zh-tw.content:
我最近開始執行一套很嚴格的飲食法，叫做「看到就吃法」。

規則只有一條：只要被我看到的食物，都算我的。
執行三個月以來，成效驚人——體重穩定上升，意志力穩定下降。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說要減肥卻每餐都拍照打卡的朋友，配一句「我們是同一派的」
variations: 進階版：閉上眼睛也不行，因為我會用聞的。
這套飲食法最大的優點是,完全不需要意志力,只需要一雙好眼睛。

en.content:
I've started a very strict diet lately. It's called the see-food diet.

There's only one rule: if I see it, it's mine.
Three months in, the results are stunning — my weight is steadily climbing and my willpower is steadily quitting.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who swears they're dieting but photographs every meal — add 'we're on the same plan'
variations: Advanced level: closing my eyes doesn't help, because then I just smell it.
The best part of this diet is it requires zero willpower — just a good pair of eyes.

items.sourceNote: 「別低頭,雙下巴會掉/皇冠會掉」勵志哏改寫成減肥版打氣語

zh-tw.content:
減肥的人請記得隨時抬頭挺胸。

不是為了氣質，是因為一低頭，雙下巴就會跑出來跟大家打招呼。
抬著頭，至少還能假裝那只是燈光問題。
zh-tw.editorNote: 減肥週拍合照前傳到群組，提醒大家統一抬頭，效果立現
variations: 抬頭挺胸的真相：那不是自信，那是物理上的必要。
低頭看手機久了你會發現,雙下巴比你還黏手機。

en.content:
If you're on a diet, remember to keep your chin up at all times.

Not for the confident posture — it's because the second you look down, your double chin pops out to say hi to everyone.
Chin up, and at least you can pretend it's just the lighting.
en.editorNote: Drop in the group chat before a diet-week group photo — tell everyone to keep their chins up, instant results
variations: The truth about good posture: that's not confidence, that's physical necessity.
Look at your phone too long and you'll realize your double chin is even more attached to it than you are.
